description: Synthetic cross-track candidate cross-reference fixture for development tests. Cross-references are operational metadata only and do not modify candidate scoring or pathway routing.
disclaimer: Cross-track references are operational cross-reference metadata only. They do not modify candidate posteriors, false-positive probability, or pathway routing, and they are not detections, discoveries, or external validation.

blocking_issues: Independent reviewers required for each contributing track
Cross-track timing alignment not yet verified
negative_evidence: Radio packet records strong RFI overlap warning
Infrared packet shows partial confusion-flag coverage
references.notes: Two independently-scored candidates share the same target ID; neither score is boosted by this cross-reference.

blocking_issues: Anomaly track flags likely catalog mismatch
negative_evidence: Both tracks report strong false-positive evidence
No surviving positive evidence after independent vetting
references.notes: Both contributing tracks recommend conservative rejection; the cross-reference preserves the disagreement rather than resolving it.

blocking_issues: No radio coverage available for this target
No infrared coverage available for this target
negative_evidence: Single-track evidence remains weak without cross-track corroboration
references.notes: Recorded so the absence of cross-track support is auditable rather than silent.

negative_evidence: SIMBAD-style match indicates a known annotated source class
Both tracks consistent with the catalog identification
references.notes: Cross-reference confirms known-object annotation rather than a new candidate.
